layman_headline: Fear grips the crypto market — patience beats impulsiveness right now.
layman_explanation: The crypto market is in a 'Fear' state with most altcoins bleeding against Bitcoin. BTC dominance at 56.2% means money is flowing out of smaller coins and into Bitcoin. While a few candidates like ZRO are approaching oversold territory, none of them have strong enough chart setups or clear catalysts to justify risking capital right now. The smart move is to wait for cleaner entries.
layman_action: Stay in cash and watch. The market hasn't given a clear buy signal yet. Wait for fear to deepen further or for a strong reversal pattern to form before committing money.
expert_reasoning: No candidate presents a compelling swing setup this cycle. ZRO is the closest with RSI at 31 and sitting on $0.90 support, but chart bias is -0.70 (bearish), price is below all MAs, and there's no bullish candlestick reversal pattern or volume breakout to confirm a bounce. GRASS dropped 28% in 7 days but has a bearish engulfing candle and -0.90 chart bias — classic falling knife. LDO rallied 20% in 7 days making it extended. BTC dominance at 56.2% is hostile to alt positions. The Fear & Greed improving from 23 to 26 is encouraging but insufficient to call a bottom. Discipline demands we wait for confirmation rather than catch knives.
risk_factors: BTC dominance at 56.2% continues to crush altcoin performance — any new alt position faces this structural headwind
Fear & Greed at 26 is improving but could easily deteriorate if BTC loses $64K support, dragging alts down further
All swing candidates are below key moving averages with bearish chart structures — no confirmed reversals yet
watch_list: ZRO holding $0.90 support — a bounce with volume would create a clean entry setup
BTC price action around $64K — a break above $66K could ease selling pressure on alts
Fear & Greed index trajectory — if it climbs above 30, sentiment shift could trigger alt rotation
market_context: Bitcoin is consolidating near $64.2K with modest gains, but altcoins remain under pressure as BTC dominance holds at 56.2%. The Fear & Greed index at 26 is improving slowly from last week's 23.6 average, but hasn't signaled a definitive sentiment bottom yet.
context_banner: Market fear is improving but no swing candidate has both oversold conditions AND a confirmed reversal pattern — discipline says wait.

key_signals.description: Fear & Greed at 26 improving from 23 weekly average — trend is positive but still firmly in Fear territory, historically a zone where patience is rewarded over impulsiveness.
ZRO RSI at 31 is the most oversold among candidates, approaching the 30 threshold that historically triggers bounce setups — but no confirmation candle yet.
Bitcoin described as 'nearing late stages of bear market' by Jamie Coutts — if BTC bottoms and rallies, alt rotation typically follows with a lag.
Every swing candidate is below key moving averages with bearish or neutral chart bias scores — no confirmed bullish reversals anywhere on the board.
ETH derivatives reset noted alongside DePIN/gaming rebound narrative — could signal early rotation but needs price confirmation before acting.
